title: malaxis grandiflora
intro: Malaxis grandiflora, also known as the large-flowered malaxis or green adder's mouth, is a type of orchid native to North America, particularly in the eastern and central regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily its ability to treat respiratory issues and reduce inflammation due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malaxis grandiflora include alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Malaxis grandiflora typically involve drying the plant's leaves and flowers, which can then be infused in hot water to make a tea or used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
